response.justification: Strong candidate with 15+ years of full-stack experience spanning multiple languages, paradigms, and industries well above the 6-year bar. The current role at re:cinq directly covers the AI agent development requirement — a differentiating and increasingly important skill — alongside TypeScript and Node.js depth. TDD and software craftsmanship practices are consistently demonstrated across roles and reinforced through teaching. The main gaps are NestJS (not mentioned despite being primary framework) and Kotlin (absent entirely), and React exposure appears limited to one role. These are meaningful but learnable gaps given the candidate's polyglot track record and architectural breadth.
strengths: Active AI agent and MCP framework development at current role directly matches the AI agent experience requirement
15+ years of polyglot full-stack delivery across Node.js, TypeScript, Java, C++, and DLang demonstrates strong engineering fundamentals and adaptability
Deep, consistent TDD culture: authored test frameworks (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), ran TDD/refactoring workshops, and applied craftsmanship practices (pairing, trunk-based dev) across multiple employers
concerns: NestJS is entirely absent from the resume despite being listed as a primary stack requirement
Kotlin is not mentioned anywhere, leaving the secondary backend technology requirement uncovered
React surface area appears limited to a single role (Optilyz); frontend depth is unclear relative to a Senior Fullstack role with equal frontend emphasis

key_factors.factor: Breadth of full-stack and polyglot experience
